rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrochemical radical tandem cyclization of N-aryl acrylamides with alkyl bromides using (TMS)3SiOH as halogen-atom transfer (XAT) reagent to access 3,3-disubstituted oxindoles bearing a CF3 group.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation regenerates the excited-state photocatalyst (PQ); the photocatalyst oxidizes (TMS)3SiOH to (TMS)3SiO•, which rearranges to a silyl radical (TMS)2(TMSO)Si• that performs XAT on the alkyl bromide to generate the key alkyl radical.

key_intermediates.name: carbon radical after addition to acrylamide alkene
cyclohexadienyl radical from intramolecular aryl cyclization

electron_flow: Visible-light-excited PQ* oxidizes (TMS)3SiOH to give (TMS)3SiO•, which rearranges to a silyl radical that abstracts Br from R-Br (XAT) to generate an alkyl radical; this radical adds to the acrylamide alkene, the resulting C-radical cyclizes onto the arene, and rearomatization/oxidation delivers the oxindole while the reduced PQ is reoxidized at the anode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited; TEMPO–(2-hydroxyethyl) adduct 61 (C11H24NO2+, m/z 202.1803) detected by HRMS, indicating intermediacy of the alkyl radical derived from 2-bromoethanol.

probe_reagents.name: (bromomethyl)cyclopropane (63)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened cyclization product 64 obtained in 48% yield, confirming a freely diffusing alkyl radical.

probe_reagents.name: 6-bromohex-1-ene (65)
probe_reagents.observation: Radical dicyclization product 66 (with cyclopentylmethyl pendant) obtained in 51% yield, consistent with 5-exo-trig cyclization of the hexenyl radical.

probe_reagents.name: (TMS)3SiOH (no R-Br control)
probe_reagents.observation: In the absence of alkyl bromide, silyl-incorporated oxindole 67 was isolated in 37% yield, supporting formation of (TMS)3SiO• and its rearrangement to a silyl radical that adds to the acrylamide.

notes: The silyl-trapping experiment in the absence of R-Br (product 67) is key evidence for the proposed Brook-type rearrangement of (TMS)3SiO• to (TMS)2(TMSO)Si• as the active XAT reagent. No DFT, CV, or KIE data presented on these pages.
